_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Evaluate extent to which collected information and/or produced intelligence satisfy information requests.
reference_document_element_description: Ability to select the appropriate analytical method for a given business question, balancing statistical rigor, time constraints, data availability, and audience comprehension capacity.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI AB domain. AB-A-002 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AB-T-001 (AB, cosine: 0.473), LS-K-010 (LS, cosine: 0.450).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.0/10 (raw 0.397). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [collected, evaluate, extent, information, intelligence, produced] vs WIDAI-only terms [ability, analytical, appropriate, audience, availability, balancing] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.840 (P=0.836, R=0.844). Balanced P/R — the 12-word DCWF element and 23-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.5%). "Evaluate extent to which collected information and/or produced intelligence sati" and "Ability to select the appropriate analytical method for a given business questio" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.548 > STS 0.397 (gap +0.151). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [none] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 6.1%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [collected, evaluate, extent, information, intelligence, produced]. WIDAI terms: [ability, analytical, appropriate, audience, availability, balancing].
